name: Mesut Ozdag, Ph.D.
title: Assistant Professor of CS, FinTech, Digital Forensics.

education.institution: University of Central Florida, Orlando, FL
education.specialization: Deep Learning, Adversarial Attacks, Medical Image Analysis

education.institution: University of Central Florida, Orlando, FL

achievements: Faculty of Computer Science.
IE Assessment Coordinator: CS MS, CS Ph.D., FinTech MS.
Chair of KAPGEM IT Policy Committee.
Publications: Paper at IEEE IGARSS conference 2026, Academic book at the Nobel Publishing Group.
2 research projects under TUBITAK 2209-A.
Joined the Erasmus+ KA131 Staff Mobility Program.
Faculty of CS, Digital Forensics and FinTech Master’s degree programs at UCF.
Created brand-new lecture materials for: COT 5480 (Data Structures), COT 6481 (Machine Learning), CAP 5619 (AI for FinTech).
Taught: COT 3100, CDA 3103, CNT 4403, CNT 4704, CAP 4102, CGS 5131, CNT 5805, CNT 6418.
Publication: 'Ai-Driven Vulnerability Analysis in Smart Contracts', IJAIA, May 2025.
Developed DL models for body parts classification, Pneumonia classification, and MRI brain segmentation.
Implemented MRI Quality QC classification and Federated Learning using AWS.
API testing implemented using Pytest library.
Pathology image analysis (H&E-stained images) for cancer prognosis prediction.
Developed Nuclei segmentation framework using Dynamic U-Net architecture.
Applied Pyradiomics features (morphological, texture, GLCM) for DLBCL detection.
Ph.D. dissertation: Adversarial attacks on DL models by natural and digital perturbations.
Publication 1: 'Adversarial Attacks and Defenses against Deep Neural Networks: A Survey' (Procedia CS, 2018).
Publication 2: 'On the Susceptibility of Deep Neural Networks to Natural Perturbations' (IJCAI 2019) - Selected BEST PAPER CANDIDATE.
Project: 'Directed Adversarial Attacks on Fingerprints using Attributions' (ICB 2019) with RBC Bank. VeriFinger SDK matching score >250.
Teaching: Recitations for Computer Logic & Organization, C Programming, and Systems Software.
Created end-to-end model for Vessel Centerline Tracing for 3D medical volumes (Aorta/Vertebral/Carotid).
Utilized Deep Reinforcement Learning with CNNs (Theano and Lasagne).
Unit, integration, acceptance and system testing using regression analysis, SQL and JIRA.
Developing test plans/scripts and passed to live version.
